pr-510.title: hotfix(rpc-s1): fix syntax error breaking every repo-connect button
pr-510.rootCause: missing closing brace in rpcSubmitCreate/rpcSubmitConnect's try block broke the ENTIRE inline <script> tag, silently making every button handler undefined
pr-510.regressionTest: IT4 in tests/check-rpc-s1-connect-repo.js — vm.Script-compiles the extracted inline script body

pr-511.title: hotfix: fix JSONB rollup fields crashing product view on real sync
pr-511.rootCause: _renderProductView called JSON.parse() unconditionally on 5 JSONB columns (health_counts, test_coverage, ac_coverage, taxonomy, dod_status_counts); pg auto-parses JSONB to objects, so this crashed with SyntaxError: "[object Object]" is not valid JSON the first time any product completed a real sync round-trip on staging
pr-511.fix: added _parseJsonbField() helper in products.js, only parses if value is actually a string
pr-511.regressionTest: new test in tests/check-pr-s2-products-route.js using object-shaped (real pg) mock values end-to-end through handleGetProductView

pr-513.title: hotfix(pr-s5..pr-s7): fall back to story.id when story.slug is absent in rollup breakdowns
pr-513.status: OPEN, CI initially failed on an unrelated pre-existing check-workspace-state.js schema break introduced by this same session's earlier state.json rewrite (fixed in a follow-up commit on this branch, see below) — re-check CI before merging
pr-513.rootCause: computeTestCoverageRollup/computeAcCoverageRollup/computeTaxonomyRollup in product-rollup.js pushed slug: story.slug with no fallback; 17 real stories in this repo's pipeline-state.json have id but no slug
pr-513.fix: all 3 call sites now use story.slug || story.id
pr-513.regressionTest: 3 new tests in tests/check-pr-s2-product-rollup.js, one per function; also confirmed 0 blank entries against a real read of this repo's own pipeline-state.json (was 17)

openFindings.summary: Test-coverage and AC-coverage per-feature lists show blank ': 100%' entries with no story name
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED — computeTestCoverageRollup() and computeAcCoverageRollup() in src/web-ui/modules/product-rollup.js (around lines 128-133 and 187-192) push `slug: story.slug` with no fallback. 17 real stories in this repo's own .github/pipeline-state.json (e.g. ougl.1 through ougl.9) have an `id` field but no `slug` field, confirmed via a direct node script scan.
openFindings.fix: APPLIED — all 3 rollup functions (test coverage, AC coverage, taxonomy) now use story.slug || story.id. Confirmed against a real read of this repo's own pipeline-state.json: 0 blank entries remaining (was 17). Shipped as PR #513.
openFindings.status: FIXED IN CODE, PR #513 OPEN — CI initially failed on an unrelated pre-existing schema break in this session's own earlier state.json rewrite (fixed via a follow-up commit, see hotfixes-shipped-without-full-story-artefacts.pr-513 above); re-check CI status and merge when green

openFindings.summary: /admin/credits returns {"error":"Forbidden"} for the account owner (heymishy) on wuce-staging
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED — admin role is seeded via ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS env var (comma-separated GitHub logins) at server.js line ~317; this var was never set on wuce-staging (same class of gap as the earlier missing PLATFORM_TENANT_ID).
openFindings.fix: APPLIED AND DEPLOYED — ran `flyctl secrets set ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS=heymishy --app wuce-staging`, confirmed via a fresh fly log read after the machine woke from autosuspend: `[arl-s4] admin role seeded for 1 login(s)`.
openFindings.status: FIXED AND DEPLOYED — user has NOT yet re-tested /admin/credits live to confirm the 403 is gone; worth confirming, and worth retrying the F5 'New feature' flow afterward in case a credit top-up resolves it too

openFindings.summary: Some epic names render garbled, e.g. 'Governance extractability ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â enforcement selection'
openFindings.rootCause: CONFIRMED NOT a rendering/code bug — grepped .github/pipeline-state.json directly and the mangled string is baked into the SOURCE DATA itself (double-encoded UTF-8, most likely an em-dash that went through the wrong encoding twice at some earlier point when this file was written/edited).
openFindings.fix: Not a code fix — would require a data cleanup pass on the stored feature.name strings in pipeline-state.json. Cosmetic, low priority.
openFindings.status: root-caused, out of scope for a code hotfix, needs a data-cleanup decision from the user (do it / leave it)

openFindings.summary: The per-feature test-coverage/AC-coverage list is a single unsorted flat list of 115+ story codes with no grouping — very hard to read, unlike the properly-grouped 'Epics' section rendered further down the same page
openFindings.rootCause: Not a bug — a design/UX gap. The Epics section already groups by epic via computeTaxonomyRollup(); the per-feature coverage breakdown does not reuse that grouping.
openFindings.fix: Needs a design decision, not a hotfix — e.g. group the per-feature breakdown by epic the same way, or collapse to just the blended % with an expandable details view for the full per-story list.
openFindings.status: flagged as design debt, not actioned, no fix proposed yet

openFindings.summary: Clicking 'New feature' now correctly routes to a skill-session chat (jrf-s1's fix confirmed working for routing), but the actual skill invocation fails with a generic 'Error — please try again' in the chat UI
openFindings.rootCause: PARTIALLY TRACED, NOT CONFIRMED — the exact client-facing string traces to src/web-ui/routes/skills.js's SSE error handler (~line 4149, inside a try/catch around the real LLM streaming call at ~line 4146). That catch block also logs a structured pino line: `_turnLog.error({ event: 'sse_error', error_message: ... })`. The user's pasted fly-log excerpt did NOT include this specific 'sse_error' log line — only a tangential PostHog isFeatureEnabled deprecation warning, an [auth] login line, and an autosuspend/suspend event (at 21:27:23, AFTER the 21:24:56 error attempt, so probably not the direct cause). Leading hypotheses, none confirmed: (a) zero credit balance (balance=0 confirmed earlier in the session via a credits_balance_check log line) causing a downstream throw rather than a clean guard block; (b) a missing/bad ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or model config on wuce-staging specifically; (c) an unrelated real API error.
openFindings.fix: NEEDS MORE INFO — ask the user to pull the exact fly log around the failing attempt's timestamp and grep specifically for 'sse_error' to get the real error_message field. Also worth retrying after F2's ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS fix + a manual credit top-up via /admin/credits, in case F2 and F5 are entangled (zero balance somehow reaching the LLM call instead of being cleanly blocked upstream).
openFindings.status: root cause NOT confirmed — this is the most important open item to resume with, since it's the one item where the actual root cause is still unknown
